channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: England Fans Navigate Post-Match Recovery: Pubs, Schools, and Workplaces Adapt to Late-Night World Cup Viewing
item.description: Following England's thrilling 5-2 victory over Mexico in a late-night World Cup clash, the nation grappled with the aftermath of an all-night viewing session. The match, which ended at 4am, prompted s...

item.title: Calgary Suburban Office Vacancy Rates Drop Significantly
item.description: Calgary's suburban office market is experiencing a notable decrease in vacancy rates, signaling a potential recovery in the real estate sector. According to recent reports, the decline in vacant offic...

item.title: First Battery's 165 Worker Retrenchments Spark Union Criticism Over Management Decisions
item.description: Metair subsidiary First Battery’s decision to retrench 165 workers has ignited a heated debate between the company and the National Union of Metalworkers of South Africa (Numsa). Numsa general secreta...

item.title: AI Agent Gains Digital Identity Through Email Integration
item.description: The author describes the process of granting their AI agent, Hermes, a digital identity by providing it with an email address. This allows the AI to interact more independently with external services,...

item.title: US-China tensions escalate over nuclear buildup and regional security concerns
item.description: The article details ongoing geopolitical tensions between the US and China following a recent missile test in the Pacific. Acting Prime Minister Richard Marles stated the test was not directly linked ...

item.title: Pakistan mediates between Libya's rival factions to promote unity
item.description: Pakistan has initiated secret diplomatic efforts to mediate between Libya's eastern and western power centers, a move that could elevate its regional influence. This initiative follows months of U.S.-...

item.title: Vice President Sara Duterte Visits Arrested Senator Rodante Marcoleta at PNP Hospital
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte visited Senator Rodante Marcoleta at the Philippine National Police General Hospital (PNPGH) in Quezon City on July 7, 2026. The visit followed Marcoleta's arrest earlier t...

item.title: England's Jordan Henderson Injures Wrist During World Cup Celebration, Opting for Surgery
item.description: England's Jordan Henderson suffered a wrist fracture during a celebratory jump over an advertising board after his team's 3-2 victory over Mexico in the 2026 World Cup Round of 16. The 36-year-old mid...

item.title: Harry Kane praises England's determination after hard-fought 3-2 World Cup win over Mexico
item.description: England captain Harry Kane praised the fighting spirit of his teammates after they secured a dramatic 3-2 victory over Mexico in the Round of 16 of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The win sent England into t...

item.title: Solstice Shares Drop Following $12 Billion Acquisition of Element
item.description: The stock price of Solstice, a renewable energy company, experienced a significant decline after announcing its intention to acquire Element, a solar technology firm, for $12 billion. The deal, which ...

item.title: Young Australians Navigate Changing Attitudes Toward Sex and Relationships
item.description: The article explores shifting sexual behaviours among younger Australians, highlighting factors like increased singledom, technology replacing face-to-face interaction, and lower alcohol consumption. ...

item.title: Supreme Court overturns convictions of MQM workers in Baldia factory fire case
item.description: The Supreme Court of Pakistan has ruled in favor of overturning the convictions of two Muttahida Qaumi Movement (MQM) workers, Abdul Rehman alias Bhola and Zubair alias Chariya, in connection with the...

item.title: NIA files chargesheet against Hafiz Saeed for alleged role in planning the 2025 Pahalgam terror attack
item.description: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) has filed a second chargesheet naming Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) founder Hafiz Mohammad Saeed as the alleged mastermind behind the April 2025 Pahalgam terror attack,...

item.title: Calgary City Reports 70 Noise Complaints During 2026 Stampede
item.description: The City of Calgary has received 70 noise complaints related to events at the 2026 Calgary Stampede, with the Cowboys Music Festival accounting for 40 of these complaints. This marks an increase from ...

item.title: Kensington toddler critically injured in gang shooting
item.description: Two-year-old Emilio Lindeman Junior was shot in the arm and collarbone by unidentified gunmen in Kensington's Glider Crescent on Saturday night. The toddler, known as Junior, was in his mother's arms ...

item.title: Family searches for missing after boat accident in B.C. waters
item.description: Ashley Lin, mother of 23-year-old charter boat captain Ming Chen, shared details about her son's death after his 30-foot vessel sank off Richmond, B.C., on June 28, 2026. Chen, described as kind-heart...

item.title: Philippines' Inflation Remains Above Target Despite June Easing to 6.4%
item.description: The Philippines' inflation rate slowed to 6.4% in June 2026, down from 6.8% in May, but still above the government's target range of 2% to 4%. The Philippine Statistics Authority reported that lower f...

item.title: Maine Senate Candidate Faces Sexual Assault Accusations
item.description: Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner is facing serious allegations of sexual assault, according to a report by Politico. Jenny Racicot, a 41-year-old Maine resident, claims that Platner entered ...

item.title: China's Shift in Tactics in the West Philippine Sea and Its Implications
item.description: The article discusses China's evolving tactics in the West Philippine Sea, highlighting a shift from aggressive actions to more covert measures. While China has avoided direct confrontations with Phil...

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's Wedding Day: Heartbreak and Celebration
